title: SOP-SIG-01: Signal Failure Initial Response
content: Upon detection of signal failure: 1) Immediately notify Control Center. 2) Impose speed restriction on affected line. 3) Activate emergency manual operation if required. 4) Dispatch maintenance team within 30 minutes. 5) Log incident in SCADA with timestamp, signal ID, and initial conditions.

title: SOP-SIG-02: Relay Replacement Procedure
content: Relay replacement: 1) Isolate signal from power supply. 2) Document existing relay specification and resistance reading. 3) Remove relay following ESD precautions. 4) Install new relay — verify specification matches or exceeds original. 5) Apply anti-corrosion treatment to housing. 6) Test relay resistance at 20°C baseline. 7) Reconnect and functional test all indications. 8) Record replacement in SCADA metadata.

title: SOP-SIG-03: Signal Housing Seal Inspection
content: Housing seal inspection procedure: 1) Visually inspect gasket for cracks, compression set, or UV degradation. 2) Apply water spray test to all seam joints. 3) Check drainage holes are clear. 4) Inspect internal for moisture evidence. 5) Replace seal if any defect found — do not defer seal replacement.

title: SOP-SIG-04: Signal Calibration Procedure
content: Signal calibration: 1) Record ambient temperature. 2) Measure relay operating voltage at nominal temperature. 3) Check pick-up and drop-away values against specification. 4) Adjust calibration screw in 0.1 increment steps. 5) Re-test after each adjustment. 6) Document final values in maintenance record. 7) Schedule follow-up test at opposite extreme temperature.

title: SOP-MNT-01: Preventive Maintenance Schedule — Zone RG Signals
content: Zone RG (Rivergate) preventive maintenance: Monthly — visual inspection and moisture check. Quarterly — full relay resistance test, connector inspection, seal integrity test. Bi-Annual — complete housing disassembly and clean, relay replacement if resistance drift >10%. Annual — full system test including emergency operation. Post-Monsoon — mandatory inspection within 14 days.

title: SOP-EMG-01: Emergency Manual Signal Operation
content: Emergency manual operation: 1) Obtain Control Center authority. 2) Ensure correct track occupation is verified. 3) Apply emergency release key. 4) Set signal to correct indication manually. 5) Communicate signal state to all affected drivers verbally. 6) Maintain continuous communication with Control Center. 7) Do not restore automatic operation without Control Center authority.

title: SOP-WTH-01: Adverse Weather Response — Signals
content: During adverse weather events: 1) Monitor weather forecast and activate alert at 50mm/hour rainfall threshold. 2) Increase inspection frequency to 4-hourly. 3) Pre-position maintenance teams at vulnerable zones. 4) Reduce line speed by 30% on flood-prone routes. 5) Initiate post-event inspection within 4 hours of weather clearing. 6) Log all weather conditions against any concurrent incidents.

title: SOP-TRK-01: Track Ultrasonic Inspection Procedure
content: Ultrasonic inspection: 1) Deploy ultrasonic test vehicle or handheld probe at specified intervals. 2) Set sensitivity to detect defects ≥5mm. 3) Mark any indication for manual inspection. 4) All indications >15mm depth require immediate speed restriction. 5) Document all indications with GPS coordinates. 6) Re-inspect any indication within 30 days.

title: SOP-SW-01: Switch Inspection and Lubrication
content: Switch maintenance: 1) Measure switch operation time — normal range 3-7 seconds. 2) Check motor current draw — >115% of nominal requires investigation. 3) Inspect point rails for wear and crack indication. 4) Apply approved lubricant to slide chairs and all moving surfaces. 5) Check stretcher bar for cracks. 6) Verify locking force within specification. Document all readings.

title: SOP-RISK-01: Asset Risk Assessment Procedure
content: Risk assessment: 1) Collect asset health score, maintenance history, incident count, and weather exposure data. 2) Apply weighted risk formula: Health 30%, Maintenance 20%, Incidents 20%, Environment 15%, Operations 15%. 3) Score >80 triggers critical maintenance order within 24 hours. 4) Score 60-80 requires maintenance within 72 hours. 5) Document risk assessment with supporting evidence.
